sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports he's doing really good and is ready to quit smoking. He states he started smoking in high school and now smokes a pack and a half a day. He reports that he woke up within an hour of waking up and smokes his first cigarette. The patient reports increased stress levels due to work and the impending arrival of his daughter. He also reports that he tried to quit cold turkey a year ago but relapsed due to stressful work situations. The patient reports he's been trying to manage stress with meditation and recently joined a gym.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 128/88 mmHg, heart rate is 68 bpm, respiratory rate is 16 breaths per minute, and oxygen saturation is 98% on room air. A regular rate and rhythm heart exam was noted. Lung exam revealed clear and equal bilateral lungs with no expiratory wheezes or rales. Neck exam revealed no lymphadenopathy and no carotid bruits. The patient's hemoglobin A1c will be ordered for his next blood draw.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's primary problem is nicotine dependence. The patient has a history of type 2 diabetes and gout. The patient is experiencing stress.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The provider plans to start the patient with a 21 milligram nicotine patch, decreasing the dosage over time. The provider recommends setting a quit date for next Monday. The provider will be with the patient every step of the way to help them quit smoking. The provider wants the patient to consider meditation or yoga to help manage stress. The provider will order a follow-up appointment in two weeks. The provider suggests the patient keep an eye on any handouts received today.
